subject: st2131
description: Ground-truth retrieval eval for the ExamCast RAG pipeline. Each item is an exam-style query; gold_chunks are the slide-chunk IDs in the slides_st2131 Chroma collection that genuinely cover the queried material. Gold labels were anchored to chunk contents by section headers and verbatim worked examples (e.g. Example 4.13 hypergeometric balls, Example 5.8 Y=1/X^2, Example 1.11 stars-and-bars).

questions.query: How do you use the basic principle of counting to find the number of ways a multi-stage procedure can be carried out?
If four whole numbers that can be zero add up to at most twenty, how many different combinations are possible?
How do you count the number of ways to split a set of people into several equal-sized teams?
Express the number of ways to arrange r distinct objects into n cycles using Stirling numbers of the first kind s(r, n).
How many ways can n couples be seated around a round table so that no husband sits next to his own wife?
Summarize the combinatorial counting techniques introduced in the chapter, including the basic principle of counting and dividing objects into groups.
If two events are each very likely, how can you put a lower bound on the probability that both happen together?
State the definition of the conditional probability of an event A given an event B.
Use Bayes' theorem to express P(A given B) in terms of P(B given A), P(A) and P(B).
Explain conditional probability and independence of events, including how independence simplifies conditional probabilities.
When is a random variable said to be discrete?
State the properties of variance, such as Var(aX) = a^2 Var(X) and Var(aX + b).
Show that the sum of two independent binomial random variables with the same success probability is again binomial.
Prove the memoryless property of the geometric distribution: P(X > m + n | X > m) = P(X > n).
Does the difference of two independent Poisson random variables follow a Poisson distribution?
Drawing a handful of balls without replacement from an urn containing two colours, how do you find the chance of getting a given number of one colour?
Explain why P(x <= X <= x + epsilon) is approximately epsilon times f(x) for a continuous random variable.
Prove the memoryless property of the exponential distribution using the survival function G(x) = 1 - F(x).
Which distributions satisfy the memoryless property and how is it proven for each?
If a variable is exponentially distributed, how do I derive the density of its reciprocal squared?
Describe the gamma distribution that arises as the waiting time until the nth event in a Poisson process.
How do you work out the distribution of the middle value among three independent random observations?
Define what it means for X and Y to be jointly continuous random variables with a joint density function.
Two points are dropped uniformly at random on overlapping intervals; what is the probability that one exceeds the other?
Given U = g(X, Y) and V = h(X, Y), how do you find the joint pdf of U and V using the Jacobian of the transformation?
Describe the general method for finding the distribution of a transformed random variable, for both single and joint transformations.
Define the conditional expectation E(X | Y = y) for jointly distributed discrete random variables.
Use the moment generating function of a geometric random variable to find E[X^3].
State the Central Limit Theorem for the standardized sum of n iid random variables as n tends to infinity.
State the key properties of the expectation of sums of random variables and of covariance for independent variables.
